title: Gooey Sour Egyptian Olives Tagine
description: A flavorful and tangy Egyptian dish featuring olives, perfect for adventurous eaters. This recipe is suitable for a bain-marie and is made with iron teppan vessels, making it ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large iron teppan vessel over medium heat.
Add the sliced olives and cook until they start to soften, about 3 minutes.
Add the ginger paste, garlic, cumin powder, coriander powder, and red pepper flakes. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the lemon juice and sugar, and stir to combine.
Add the water and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes.
Stir in the chopped parsley and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Remove from heat and serve hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
